title: HiBob Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**HiBob** (a.k.a. **Bob**) is a modern HR / HCM platform exposing a REST API suite under `https://api.hibob.com/v1`. This connector â€” `HiBobConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "hibob"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) â€” wraps the HR surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| People (employees) | `/people`, `/people/search`, `/people/{id}` | List, search, read, create, update employees |
| Profile | `/profiles/{id}` | Humanised full-profile projection |
| Employments | `/people/{id}/employment` | Employment history per employee |
| Time-Off | `/timeoff/requests/changes`, `/timeoff/employees/{id}/requests` | List + create time-off requests |
| Payroll | `/payroll/history/{id}` | Per-employee payroll history |
| Lifecycle | `/people/lifecycle/changes` | Hire / leave / return / terminate event log |
| Departments | `/company/named-lists/department` | Department named-list |
| Sites | `/company/named-lists/site` | Office-location named-list |

The connector normalises People records into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff (3 attempts, honours `Retry-After`), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC â€” all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::HiBobHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Provision a Service User in HiBob

1. Sign in to https://app.hibob.com.
2. Open **Settings -> Integrations -> Service Users**.
3. Click **New service user**, grant the scopes you need (People read/write, Time-off read/write, Payroll read, Lifecycle read, Reports read).
4. Copy the **Service User ID** (looks like `SERVICE-12345`) and the **Service User Token** â€” Bob shows the token only once.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP -> Manage Connectors -> HiBob**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `service_user_id` (string) | The Service User ID from step 4 |
| `service_user_token` (secret) | The Service User token from step 4 |
| `base_url` (string, optional) | Override `https://api.hibob.com/v1` (rarely needed) |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number) | Client-side soft cap, default 60 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates the two credentials are non-empty (it does NOT call the API), initialises the `HiBobHTTPClient`, and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, AUTHENTICATED)`.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which lists 1 employee via `GET /people?fields=id&limit=1` as a lightweight probe. On 2xx -> `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401/403 -> `DEGRADED + INVALID_CREDENTIALS` with structured-log warning. On transport error -> `OFFLINE`.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
HiBob REST API uses **HTTP Basic authentication** for Service Users (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`). No OAuth dance, no token refresh, no expiry.

### Credential model

- `service_user_id` is sent as the HTTP Basic **username**.
- `service_user_token` is sent as the HTTP Basic **password**.
- The connector encodes them as `Authorization: Basic base64(service_user_id:service_user_token)`.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `service_user_id` + `service_user_token`. Initialises `HiBobHTTPClient`. Does **not** call any HiBob endpoint. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns a synthetic `TokenInfo(token_type="Basic")` â€” there is no token exchange. |
| `health_check()` | `GET /people?fields=id&limit=1` â€” minimal probe. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called â€” no token to refresh. |
| token storage | None. |

### Header contract

Every outbound request:

```
Authorization: Basic base64(SERVICE-12345:<token>)
Content-Type:  application/json
Accept:        application/json
```

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` â€” never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `service_user_id` | string | yes | `self.service_user_id` | Basic-Auth username |
| `service_user_token` | secret | yes | `self.service_user_token` | Basic-Auth password |
| `base_url` | string | no | `self.base_url` (defaults to `https://api.hibob.com/v1`) | Override for sandbox or proxy |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 60) | Client-side soft cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["service_user_id", "service_user_token"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_HIBOB_BASE = "https://api.hibob.com/v1"
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `HiBobConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::HiBobHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `service_user_id` + `service_user_token`; initialise `HiBobHTTPClient`. Does NOT call the API.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No-op for Service-User auth â€” returns a synthetic TokenInfo(token_type='Basic').

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Lists 1 employee via `GET /people?fields=id&limit=1`. Classifies failures via `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Iterates HiBob employees via `POST /people/search` -> `NormalizedDocument` -> `ingest_document`. Returns `SyncResult` with `documents_synced / _failed / _found`.

children.title: People (employees)
children.content:
### `async list_people(*, limit=50, fields=None) -> Dict`
`GET /people?limit={N}` â€” simple listing, optional field projection.

### `async search_people(filters=None, fields=None, *, include_humanized=False, fields_humanized=None) -> Dict`
`POST /people/search` â€” body: `{fields: [...], filters: [...]}`. When `include_humanized=True` each employee gets a tidy `humanized` projection.

### `async get_employee(employee_id) -> Dict`
`GET /people/{employee_id}`.

### `async get_employee_profile(employee_id) -> Dict`
`GET /profiles/{employee_id}` â€” humanised full-profile projection.

### `async create_employee(first_name, surname, email, work_email=None, start_date=None, company_id=None, site=None) -> Dict`
`POST /people` â€” body: `{firstName, surname, email, workEmail?, work: {startDate?, companyId?, site?}}`.

### `async update_employee(employee_id, fields) -> Dict`
`PUT /people/{employee_id}` â€” body: arbitrary fields dict.

children.title: Employments + Time-Off
children.content:
### `async list_employments(employee_id) -> Dict`
`GET /people/{employee_id}/employment` â€” employment history per employee.

### `async list_time_off_requests(from_date=None, to_date=None, policy_type_display_name=None, include_pending=True) -> Dict`
`GET /timeoff/requests/changes` â€” list time-off changes in a window.

### `async create_time_off_request(employee_id, policy_type_display_name, request_range_type, start_date, end_date=None, description=None) -> Dict`
`POST /timeoff/employees/{employee_id}/requests` â€” submit a time-off request.

children.title: Payroll Â· Lifecycle Â· Named lists
children.content:
### `async list_payroll(employee_id) -> Dict`
`GET /payroll/history/{employee_id}` â€” per-employee payroll history.

### `async list_lifecycle_changes(from_date=None, to_date=None) -> Dict`
`GET /people/lifecycle/changes` â€” hire / leave / return / terminate event log.

### `async list_departments() -> Dict`
`GET /company/named-lists/department`.

### `async list_sites() -> Dict`
`GET /company/named-lists/site`.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
HiBobError                            # base; carries status_code + response_body
â”śâ”€â”€ HiBobAuthError                    # 401 / 403
â”śâ”€â”€ HiBobBadRequestError              # 400
â”śâ”€â”€ HiBobNotFoundError                # 404
â”śâ”€â”€ HiBobConflictError                # 409 (duplicate email)
â”śâ”€â”€ HiBobRateLimitError               # 429 â€” retry_after_s
â””â”€â”€ HiBobServerError                  # 5xx
```

Back-compat aliases preserved:
```
HiBobNetworkError = HiBobServerError  # legacy name from older code
HiBobNotFound     = HiBobNotFoundError
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 / 409 | Raise immediately |
| 429 | Honours `Retry-After` header; otherwise exponential backoff (`_RETRY_BASE_DELAY_S * 2 ** attempt`) up to `_RETRY_MAX_DELAY_S=16s`. Retries up to `_MAX_RETRIES=3` |
| 5xx | Same exponential backoff, retry up to 3 |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `httpx.NetworkError` | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `HiBobNetworkError` |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps them as follows:

```
HiBobAuthError      -> ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
HiBobNetworkError   -> ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,  CONNECTED)
HiBobError (other)  -> ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)
```

The `_STATUS_MAP` class constant codifies the raw HTTP -> (health, auth_status) mapping for monitoring overlays:

```
401 -> (OFFLINE,   TOKEN_EXPIRED)
403 -> (UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
429 -> (DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `service_user_id` or `service_user_token` blank | Re-enter both in ACP. Bob shows the token only once at generation â€” rotate the Service User if lost. |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 401/403 from HiBob â€” Service User disabled or lacks scope | In Bob -> Settings -> Integrations -> Service Users, edit the Service User and grant the required scopes (People read, Time-off read, etc.). |
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, CONNECTED)` at health_check | Network / DNS failure reaching `api.hibob.com` | Check egress allowlist (see `vault_egress_allowlist_prod_requirement.md`). HiBob isn't on the default allowlist. |
| `hibob.http.retry` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (HiBob per-minute rate cap) | The client honours `Retry-After`. Lower `rate_limit_per_min` or batch fewer requests. |
| `HiBobNotFound` on `get_employee` | The employee was deleted or the id was renamed during a rehire | Re-run `search_people()` to refresh the directory; ids are stable across renames but can change after deletion. |
| `HiBobConflictError` on `create_employee` | Duplicate email in Bob | Bob enforces unique `email` across the tenant. Pick a different email or update the existing record. |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'HiBobNetworkError'` | Older `exceptions.py` without back-compat aliases | Already fixed â€” `HiBobNetworkError = HiBobServerError` alias added in `exceptions.py`. |
